summary.intent: First, verify the new session spawn workflow end-to-end. Then implement tab persistence so open tabs survive app restarts, restoring session tabs on startup.
summary.outcome: The spawn workflow was confirmed working correctly. Tab persistence was implemented using localStorage in TabsProvider, restoring session and branchLog tabs (excluding spawn tabs) on startup.

repo: New session workflow: ActiveSessions → openSpawnTab (TabsContext) → SpawnSessionScreen polls for session by timestamp while EmbeddedTerminal sends the claude command to PTY
SpawnSessionScreen uses a ±5s timestamp window (spawnedAt - 5s) to match a newly created session from the server
Tab types include 'session', 'branchLog', and 'spawn' — spawn tabs are transient and should not be persisted
Server PTY terminal support is on ws://localhost:3000?type=terminal&cwd=<cwd>, with a 300ms delay before sending commands
Tab titles are updated via updateTabTitle using intent ?? summary ?? prompt.slice(0,40) once a session is found

code.finding: handleSpawn escapes the prompt and calls openSpawnTab(cwd, "claude '<prompt>'", title)
openSpawnTab creates a spawn tab with spawnedAt = Date.now() and immediately switches to it
Polls fetchSessions() every 2s plus WS updates, renders spinner until session found, then shows SessionDetail in an inner MemoryRouter
PTY shell spawning and bidirectional I/O streaming over WebSocket; hooks write to SQLite and WS broadcast notifies UI

workflow: localStorage was chosen over a backend/SQLite approach for tab persistence to avoid API changes — simpler and sufficient for client-side state
Spawn tabs are intentionally excluded from persistence since they represent in-flight session creation, not stable session views

open_items: Tab persistence uses localStorage — if sessions are deleted server-side, stale tabs may be restored on startup with no corresponding session

Multiple files were modified (Shell.tsx, TabsContext.tsx, EmbeddedTerminal.tsx, SessionRow.tsx, ActiveSessions.tsx, LauncherScreen.tsx, SessionDetail.tsx, server.ts) suggesting the persistence feature had broader ripple effects worth reviewing
